title: Today's AI Brief: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
summary: A weekly AI news brief with Leo Stone
imageAlt: Image from source article for How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageCaption: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment
imageSourceTitle: How the Pope’s Magnifica Humanitas offers a template for individuals to meet the AI moment

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to my weekly AI brief. I've been digging into some interesting developments in the world of artificial intelligence, and I'm excited to share them with you today.

First up, the Pope has weighed in on AI with a new encyclical called Magnifica Humanitas. Now, I'm not one to usually get my news from the Vatican, but this one's got some real teeth. The Pope's statement that 'Technology is never neutral' caught my eye. It's a reminder that AI isn't just a tool, but a force that can shape our values and society. I'm curious to see how technologists and policymakers respond to this.

Next, Google's making some significant investments in Missouri. They're building a new data center in Montgomery County, which is great news for the local economy. What's interesting here is that they're focusing on responsible expansion, energy affordability, and creating new career pathways. It's a big deal for the region, and I'm glad to see companies thinking about the broader impact of their growth.

I'm also keeping an eye on how AI is changing the way we think about data boundaries and safety. As AI becomes more pervasive, we're going to have to get serious about protecting our information and making sure these systems are trustworthy. It's a complex problem, but it's one we need to tackle head-on.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
